title: Biometric Authentication Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of biometric auth, Secure Enclave, SecureStore, and edge case handling in React Native.

questions.question: When a user authenticates with Face ID in your React Native app, does your app code receive the raw facial scan data?

options.text: Yes — the raw biometric data is sent to your callback function so you can verify it against your server
No — the biometric template is stored and processed exclusively in the device's Secure Enclave hardware; your app only receives a boolean success/failure result
Yes — but it is encrypted with the user's PIN before being passed to the app
Only on Android — iOS sends biometric data to apps via the Keychain

questions.question: What does `SecureStore.setItemAsync(key, value, { requireAuthentication: true })` do?

options.text: Immediately prompts the user for biometric authentication before saving the value
Saves the value protected by the hardware keystore — the biometric prompt will be required when reading the value with getItemAsync
Encrypts the value with the user's PIN and stores it in AsyncStorage
Registers the value as requiring a fresh login from the server every time it is accessed

questions.question: What should your app do when LocalAuthentication returns `error: 'lockout'`?

options.text: Delete the user account as the device may be compromised
Retry biometric authentication automatically after a 2-second delay
Inform the user that too many attempts failed and direct them to unlock the device with their PIN/pattern first
Enable biometric bypass mode and allow access without authentication

questions.question: Why should biometric authentication be offered as opt-in rather than mandatory?

options.text: Mandatory biometrics are prohibited by GDPR in Europe
Not all devices support biometrics, not all users enroll them, and biometrics can fail — mandatory biometric login would lock out users from their accounts
Opt-in biometrics are faster than mandatory ones due to how the Secure Enclave scans
Mandatory biometrics prevent the app from being submitted to the App Store

questions.question: What is the correct moment to offer the user the option to enable biometric login?

options.text: During app onboarding before the user has created an account
After the user's first successful password login — they now have a valid session and can opt-in to biometric convenience for future logins
Every time the app opens, to ensure the user hasn't changed their mind
Only in device Settings, not within the app
